data.content:
This is an **Android tombstone** from a .NET MAUI app (`com.companyname.MauiTest`). Here's my analysis:

**Crash Summary:**
- **Signal**: SIGABRT — abort message: `"not compatible"`
- **Memory hint**: `"some intentional crash on startup."`
- **Runtime**: Mono (`libmonosgen-2.0.so`), ABI: arm64
- **BuildId**: `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`

**Identified .NET runtime frames** (frames #01–#07 in `libmonosgen-2.0.so`), plus frame #08 in anonymous/JIT memory.

Let me try to fetch debug symbols from the Microsoft symbol server and symbolicate those frames.

arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; echo "---"; which readelf objdump 2>/dev/null

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
# Try Microsoft symbol server - ELF buildid format
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
FILENAME="libmonosgen-2.0.so"

# Try several URL formats
echo "=== Attempt 1: elf-buildid-sym format ==="
curl -sSL -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" -o /dev/null "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}"

echo "=== Attempt 2: elf-buildid format ==="
curl -sSL -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" -o /dev/null "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}"

echo "=== Attempt 3: raw buildid ==="
curl -sSL -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" -o /dev/null "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}"

echo "=== Attempt 4: _.debug format ==="
curl -sSL -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" -o /dev/null "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug"
arguments.description: Try downloading symbols from Microsoft symbol server

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Try downloading symbols from Microsoft symbol server

data.reasoningText:
This is an Android tombstone/crash log (not an Apple .ips crash log), so the `apple-crash-symbolication` skill is not appropriate here. Let me analyze this myself.

This is a .NET MAUI app crash on Android. The crash is a SIGABRT with the message "not compatible". The backtrace shows frames in `libmonosgen-2.0.so` (the Mono runtime used by .NET on Android).

Key details:
- **App**: `com.companyname.MauiTest`
- **Signal**: SIGABRT (signal 6)
- **Abort message**: "not compatible"


- **ABI**: arm64
- **Runtime library**: `libmonosgen-2.0.so` with BuildId `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`

The crash appears to be triggered intentionally on startup based on the memory contents. Most of the stack frames are in the Mono runtime itself (frames #01-#07), with frame #08 likely being JIT-compiled managed code in anonymous memory. To properly symbolicate these frames, I'd need the debug symbols for the specific build of `libmonosgen-2.0.so`.

For Android, I can try fetching symbols from Microsoft's symbol server using the BuildId, then use tools like `llvm-symbolizer` or `addr2line` to map the addresses to actual function names and source locations. I should verify the exact URL format Microsoft uses for their symbol server with ELF binaries, then try a few different approaches—starting with downloading directly from their server, falling back to debuginfod if needed, and checking whether tools like llvm-symbolizer or addr2line are available on the system.

arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; echo "---"; which readelf objdump 2>/dev/null

# Try Microsoft symbol server - ELF buildid format
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
FILENAME="libmonosgen-2.0.so"

# Try several URL formats
echo "=== Attempt 1: elf-buildid-sym format ==="
curl -sSL -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" -o /dev/null "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}"

echo "=== Attempt 2: elf-buildid format ==="
curl -sSL -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" -o /dev/null "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}"

echo "=== Attempt 3: raw buildid ==="
curl -sSL -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" -o /dev/null "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}"

echo "=== Attempt 4: _.debug format ==="
curl -sSL -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" -o /dev/null "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug"
arguments.description: Try downloading symbols from Microsoft symbol server

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "which llvm-symbolizer addr2line llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; echo \"---\"; which readelf objdump 2>/dev/null", "description": "Check available symbolication tools"}

toolCalls.name: bash

echo "=== Attempt 2: elf-buildid format ==="
curl -sSL -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" -o /dev/null "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}"

echo "=== Attempt 3: raw buildid ==="
curl -sSL -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" -o /dev/null "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}"

echo "=== Attempt 4: _.debug format ==="
curl -sSL -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" -o /dev/null "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug"", "description": "Try downloading symbols from Microsoft symbol server"}

toolArgs.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; echo "---"; which readelf objdump 2>/dev/null

# Try Microsoft symbol server - ELF buildid format
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
FILENAME="libmonosgen-2.0.so"

echo "=== Symbolicating .NET runtime frames ==="
for addr in 0x222098 0x2241ac 0x1abc80 0x1abd14 0x16dfa4 0x16db6c 0x16de04; do
  echo -n "  pc ${addr}: "
  addr2line -f -e _.debug -C "${addr}" 2>&1 | paste - - 
done

data.content:
Here's the **symbolicated backtrace**:

| Frame | Address | Function | Source |
|-------|---------|----------|--------|
| #00 | `0x6ae1c` | `abort` | libc.so |
| #01 | `0x222098` | `ves_icall_System_Environment_FailFast` | icall.c:6244 |
| #02 | `0x2241ac` | `ves_icall_System_Environment_FailFast_raw` | icall-def.h:202 |
| #03 | `0x1abc80` | `do_icall` | interp.c:2457 |
| #04 | `0x1abd14` | `get_interp_to_native_trampoline` | interp.c:1732 |
| #05 | `0x16dfa4` | `handle_isinst` | type-checking.c:? |
| #06 | `0x16db6c` | `handle_isinst` | type-checking.c:506 |
| #07 | `0x16de04` | `handle_isinst` | type-checking.c:528 |
| #08 | `0x307c` | *(JIT-compiled managed code)* | anonymous memory |

**Root cause**: The app called `Environment.FailFast("not compatible")` during startup. The call chain shows `handle_isinst` (a type-compatibility check) leading to the fail-fast, with the memory near x14 confirming: *"some intentional crash on startup."*

Frame #08 is JIT-compiled managed code (anonymous memory) — it can't be symbolicated from native symbols. To resolve it, you'd need the managed stack trace from `adb logcat` or Mono's managed crash output.
